primary_meaning: a thin tool used for writing or drawing
usage_notes: Made of wood with graphite inside
common_collocations: pencil case, pencil sharpener, yellow pencil
image_context: A yellow pencil lying on an open notebook ready for writing
example_1: I need a pencil to write.
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de um lápis para escrever.
example_3: Please give me a red pencil.
example_3_translation: Por favor, me dê um lápis vermelho.
memory_hook: You 'pen' with a pencil to seal your thoughts
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'pen' - a pen uses ink, a pencil uses graphite

primary_meaning: a container for carrying things
usage_notes: Comes in many sizes and materials
common_collocations: shopping bag, paper bag, bag of rice
image_context: A brown paper shopping bag with handles on a table
example_1_translation: Eu tenho uma bolsa nova.
example_3: Please put the books in the bag.
example_3_translation: Por favor, coloque os livros na bolsa.
memory_hook: You 'bag' all your things to carry them
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'bag' with 'baggage' which is heavier luggage

primary_meaning: items worn on the body
usage_notes: Always plural in English, never 'a clothes'
common_collocations: new clothes, warm clothes, dirty clothes
image_context: Colorful clothes hanging neatly in a open closet
example_1: I need new clothes for school.
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de roupas novas para a escola.
example_3: Put your clothes in the bag.
pronunciation_tips: The 'the' sounds like 'thee' at the end
memory_hook: You 'clothe' your body with clothes
common_mistakes: Never use 'a clothes' - clothes is always plural

primary_meaning: footwear worn on the feet
usage_notes: Plural noun, one shoe is singular
common_collocations: new shoes, sports shoes, clean shoes
image_context: A clean pair of white sneakers placed neatly on the floor
example_1_translation: Meus sapatos são pretos.
example_3: Please take off your shoes.
memory_hook: You 'shoe' your feet to protect them
common_mistakes: Don't say 'a shoe' when referring to both feet - use 'shoes'

primary_meaning: a round red or green fruit
usage_notes: Common idiom: 'an apple a day keeps the doctor away'
common_collocations: red apple, apple juice, fresh apple
image_search_query: red apple on wooden table fruit
image_context: A shiny red apple sitting on a rustic wooden table
example_1: I eat an apple every day.
example_1_translation: Eu como uma maçã todos os dias.
example_3: Would you like an apple?
memory_hook: A is for Apple - the most basic fruit
common_mistakes: Starts with vowel sound, use 'an apple' not 'a apple'

primary_meaning: a food made from flour and baked
usage_notes: Uncountable - use 'a piece of bread' or 'a loaf of bread'
common_collocations: fresh bread, bread and butter, slice of bread
image_search_query: fresh loaf of bread on cutting board
image_context: A freshly baked golden loaf of bread on a wooden cutting board
example_1_translation: Eu compro pão toda manhã.
example_3: I want a slice of bread.
example_3_translation: Eu quero uma fatia de pão.
memory_hook: You 'spread' butter on bread
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a bread', say 'a piece of bread'

primary_meaning: a white liquid produced by cows
usage_notes: Uncountable - use 'a glass of milk'
common_collocations: fresh milk, glass of milk, cold milk
image_context: A tall cold glass of fresh white milk on a kitchen table
example_1_translation: Eu bebo leite toda manhã.
example_2: The milk is in the fridge.
example_3: Can I have a glass of milk?
example_3_translation: Posso tomar um copo de leite?
memory_hook: Babies 'milk' from their mothers
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a milk', say 'a glass of milk'

primary_meaning: an oval object produced by birds
usage_notes: Chicken eggs are most common
common_collocations: boiled egg, fried egg, egg and rice
image_context: A brown chicken egg sitting peacefully in a straw nest
example_1_translation: Eu quero um ovo cozido.
example_3: Do you eat eggs for breakfast?
example_3_translation: Você come ovos no café da manhã?
memory_hook: E for Egg - simple and basic
common_mistakes: Starts with vowel sound - use 'an egg' not 'a egg'

primary_meaning: an animal that lives in water
usage_notes: Same form for singular and plural
common_collocations: fresh fish, fish and chips, fried fish
image_context: A beautiful orange fish swimming gracefully in clear blue aquarium water
example_1_translation: Eu gosto de peixe para o jantar.
example_3: How many fish do you see?
memory_hook: Fish live in 'fish' water
common_mistakes: Plural is also 'fish' not 'fishes' (except for different species)

primary_meaning: a hot drink made from leaves
usage_notes: Uncountable - use 'a cup of tea'
common_collocations: cup of tea, hot tea, green tea
image_search_query: steaming cup of tea with saucer
image_context: A steaming cup of tea sitting on a white saucer with steam rising
example_1_translation: Eu bebo chá toda manhã.
example_3: The tea is very hot.
memory_hook: You 'see' tea leaves in hot tea
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a tea', say 'a cup of tea'

primary_meaning: a hot dark drink made from beans
usage_notes: Uncountable - use 'a cup of coffee'
common_collocations: cup of coffee, black coffee, hot coffee
image_search_query: white cup of hot coffee with steam
image_context: A white cup of steaming hot coffee on a wooden table
example_1_translation: Eu bebo café toda manhã.
example_3: Can I have a cup of coffee?
example_3_translation: Posso tomar uma xícara de café?
pronunciation_tips: First syllable sounds like 'off', second like 'ee'
memory_hook: You 'cough' after drinking hot coffee
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a coffee', say 'a cup of coffee'

primary_meaning: a small round container for drinking
usage_notes: Usually for hot drinks
common_collocations: cup of tea, coffee cup, tea cup
image_context: A clean white ceramic cup with a handle on a saucer
example_1: I need a cup of water.
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de um copo de água.
example_3: Please give me a clean cup.
example_3_translation: Por favor, me dê um copo limpo.
memory_hook: You cup your hands to drink
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'cup' (drinking vessel) with 'glass' (also for drinking)

primary_meaning: a tool with prongs used for eating
usage_notes: Part of cutlery set with knife and spoon
common_collocations: knife and fork, fork and spoon, table fork
image_context: A shiny silver fork placed neatly next to a dinner plate
example_1_translation: Por favor, me passe um garfo.
example_2: I need a fork to eat.
example_2_translation: Eu preciso de um garfo para comer.
example_3: This fork is clean.
memory_hook: A fork has 'for' prongs
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'knife' - fork goes in left hand

primary_meaning: a sharp tool for cutting food
usage_notes: Part of cutlery with fork and spoon
common_collocations: knife and fork, sharp knife, table knife
image_search_query: sharp kitchen knife on cutting board
image_context: A sharp kitchen knife lying on a wooden cutting board
example_1: Please be careful with the knife.
example_1_translation: Por favor, tenha cuidado com a faca.
example_2_translation: Eu preciso de uma faca afiada.
example_3: Can you pass me the knife?
example_3_translation: Você pode me passar a faca?
memory_hook: A 'knight' uses a knife in battle
common_mistakes: Plural is 'knives' not 'knifes', silent k at start

primary_meaning: a tool with a round end for eating
usage_notes: Used for soups, cereals, and liquids
common_collocations: soup spoon, wooden spoon, tea spoon
image_context: A shiny silver spoon resting on a folded white napkin
example_1: I need a spoon for the soup.
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de uma colher para a sopa.
example_2: Please give me a clean spoon.
example_2_translation: Por favor, me dê uma colher limpa.
example_3: This spoon is very small.
memory_hook: You 'spoon' feed someone with care
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'spoon' which is for liquids, fork for solids

primary_meaning: a device that shows the time
usage_notes: Can be on wall, desk, or digital
common_collocations: wall clock, alarm clock, clock on the wall
image_search_query: round white wall clock showing time
image_context: A round white wall clock with black hands showing three o'clock on a beige wall
example_1_translation: Que horas são no relógio?
example_2: The clock is on the wall.
example_3: My clock is broken.
memory_hook: A clock 'knocks' the time all day
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'clock' (device on wall) with 'watch' (worn on wrist)

primary_meaning: coins and paper notes used to buy things
usage_notes: Uncountable noun
common_collocations: make money, spend money, save money
image_search_query: pile of colorful banknotes and coins
image_context: A pile of colorful banknotes and shiny coins on a white background
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de mais dinheiro.
example_3: I don't have much money.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables, stress on first, short 'u' sound
memory_hook: You work hard for your 'many' money
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a money', use 'some money' or 'a lot of money'

primary_meaning: a metal object used to open a lock
usage_notes: Plural is 'keys'
common_collocations: house key, car key, key to the door
image_context: A gold house key hanging from a simple metal keychain
example_1_translation: Eu perdi minha chave.
example_3: Here is the key to the house.
example_3_translation: Aqui está a chave da casa.
memory_hook: You 'key' the lock to open it
common_mistakes: Plural is 'keys' not 'keyes'

primary_meaning: a round object used in sports
usage_notes: Many sports use balls
common_collocations: football, basketball, tennis ball
image_search_query: red soccer ball on green grass field
image_context: A red and white soccer ball sitting on a green grass football field
example_1: The children are playing with a ball.
example_1_translation: As crianças estão brincando com uma bola.
example_2_translation: Eu preciso de uma bola nova.
example_3: Kick the ball!
memory_hook: You 'ball' up your fists when angry
common_mistakes: Different sports have different balls - don't assume size or material

primary_meaning: sounds arranged in a pleasant way
usage_notes: Uncountable noun
common_collocations: listen to music, music lesson, pop music
image_search_query: musician playing guitar with music notes
image_context: A musician playing an acoustic guitar with colorful music notes floating in the air
example_1_translation: Eu gosto de música.
example_3: Do you listen to music every day?
example_3_translation: Você ouve música todos os dias?
memory_hook: Music makes you feel 'euphoric'
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a music', say 'a piece of music' or 'some music'

primary_meaning: music with words
usage_notes: A song has lyrics
common_collocations: pop song, love song, sing a song
image_search_query: singer performing song on stage with spotlight
image_context: A singer performing on stage under a bright spotlight with music notes floating
example_1_translation: Esta é minha música favorita.
example_3: I know many English songs.
example_3_translation: Eu sei muitas músicas em inglês.
memory_hook: When you 'sing' a song, you 'song' along
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'song' (with words) with 'music' (general sound)

primary_meaning: a large town with many buildings
usage_notes: Larger than a town
common_collocations: big city, city center, live in the city
image_search_query: tall buildings in big city skyline
image_context: Impressive tall buildings rising up in a modern big city skyline at sunset
example_1: I live in a big city.
example_1_translation: Eu moro em uma cidade grande.
example_3: She wants to visit the city.
memory_hook: A 'city' is where many 'citizens' live
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'city' (large) with 'town' (smaller)

primary_meaning: a place where children learn
usage_notes: Where formal education happens
common_collocations: go to school, school bus, school day
image_context: A colorful school building with children playing in the front yard
example_1: I go to school every day.
example_1_translation: Eu vou para a escola todos os dias.
example_3: The school is closed today.
memory_hook: In school you learn to be a 'fool' at first but then you 'school' yourself
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'school' (building) with 'school' (group of fish) - same word different meaning

primary_meaning: an electronic machine for work and entertainment
usage_notes: Used for many tasks like writing, browsing internet
common_collocations: desktop computer, laptop computer, computer screen
image_context: A modern silver laptop computer open on a clean wooden desk with screen glowing
example_1: I use a computer for work.
example_1_translation: Eu uso um computador para trabalhar.
example_3: Can I use your computer?
memory_hook: You 'compute' things on a computer
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'computer' with 'calculator' - a computer is much more powerful
